mission.title: Install and Configure Harbor on Kubernetes
mission.description: Harbor is an open-source trusted cloud-native registry project that stores, signs, and scans content. Installing Harbor allows you to manage your container images securely and efficiently with advanced features such as user management and access control.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Harbor Helm chart repository to your local Helm client using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add harbor https://helm.goharbor.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Harbor using Helm
steps.description:
Install Harbor with the specified version using Helm. Make sure to create a namespace for Harbor:
```bash
helm install harbor harbor/harbor --version v2.14.2 --namespace harbor --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if all the Harbor pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace harbor
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the values of the Harbor deployment to set resource limits. You can do this by creating a custom values file (harbor-values.yaml) and applying it:
```yaml
# harbor-values.yaml
resources:
  requests:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  limits:
    cpu: "1"
    memory: "1Gi"
```
Then apply the configuration:
```bash
helm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor --version v2.14.2 --namespace harbor -f harbor-values.yaml
```

steps.title: Access Harbor UI
steps.description:
Port-forward the Harbor service to access the UI:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/harbor-harbor-portal 8080:80 --namespace harbor
```
You can now access the Harbor UI at http://localhost:8080.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Harbor will have all pods running in the 'harbor' namespace, and you will be able to access the Harbor UI at http://localhost:8080. Additionally, resource limits will be configured as specified.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add harbor https://helm.goharbor.io
helm repo update
helm install harbor harbor/harbor --version v2.14.2 --namespace harbor --create-namespace

# harbor-values.yaml
resources:
  requests:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  limits:
    cpu: "1"
    memory: "1Gi"
helm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor --version v2.14.2 --namespace harbor -f harbor-values.yaml

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy Harbor.
